articles.title: Iran‑Israel‑US Conflict Escalates with New Strikes
articles.summary: The ongoing conflict involving Iran, Israel and US forces continued on 12 March 2026, with reports of wide‑scale Israeli strikes on Iranian targets and Hezbollah rocket fire near northern Israel. At least 17 people were injured in airstrikes on Beirut suburbs. Regional tensions remain high as violence spreads. (Xinhua)

articles.title: Global Oil Supply Concerns Amid Middle East Crisis
articles.summary: Disruptions in key shipping routes like the Strait of Hormuz due to the Middle East conflict have raised concerns about global oil supplies, potentially pushing prices higher and prompting international responses to stabilise markets. (Xinhua, Sunday Guardian Live)

articles.title: New UK Dual‑Nationality Passport Rules Cause Confusion
articles.summary: New entry rules in the UK require dual nationals to present a valid British passport or a certificate of entitlement to travel, causing uncertainty for some dual nationality holders and travellers. (ITV News reporting)

articles.title: India to Host Global Conference on Women in Agri‑Food Systems
articles.summary: India announced it will host the Global Conference on Women in Agri‑Food Systems from 12‑14 March in New Delhi to advance inclusion and strengthen women’s roles in agriculture globally. (Economic Times)

articles.title: Business Markets See Mixed Reactions on 12 March
articles.summary: Global financial markets showed mixed performance as investors reacted to geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and shifting energy forecasts amid ongoing conflict concerns. (Sunday Guardian Live)

articles.title: IPL 2026 Schedule Released Ahead of Season
articles.summary: The Indian Premier League (IPL) 2026 schedule was officially announced, with key matchups set for late March and significant prize money awarded to the recent T20 World Cup winners. (Sunday Guardian Live)

articles.title: Education Sector Expands in India
articles.summary: The Indian government approved thousands of new MBBS and postgraduate medical training seats for the 2025‑26 academic year as part of efforts to strengthen healthcare education. (Sunday Guardian Live, NDTV School Headlines)

articles.title: Turkey Launches Antitrust Action Against Audit Firms
articles.summary: The competition authority in Turkey announced an antitrust probe into 65 audit firms, including local branches of major global accounting companies, as part of regulatory scrutiny in the financial sector. (Economic Times)
